cases.title: Clef des Champs
cases.intro: A farm-to-table identity rooted in soil, season, and ceremony — built to feel as alive as the produce it represents.

sections.heading: An identity that tastes like the land
sections.body: We were asked to build a brand that holds both heritage and modernity — handmade in feel, yet able to scale across packaging, signage, and digital.

items.heading: Approach
items.body: Strategy first. We mapped the rituals of harvest into a modular grid.

items.heading: Outcome
items.body: A 30% lift in shelf recognition and a system the team can extend.

cases.title: Ditchfield
cases.intro: A heritage roastery rebrand — slow craft, deep warmth, and a wordmark built to age like the beans it sells.

sections.heading: Old soul, new shelf presence
sections.body: Ditchfield needed to feel rooted without feeling dated.

cases.title: Cinéma Cinéma
cases.intro: A repertory cinema brand that turns every screening into an event — bold, cinematic, and unapologetically loud.

sections.heading: Double feature energy
sections.body: We doubled everything — the name, the marks, the marquee.

cases.title: Sovimage
cases.intro: A production house web platform built to showcase the reel first — fast, dark, and cinematic.

cases.title: Quartier Molson
cases.intro: A neighbourhood brand for a former brewery district — industrial heritage reframed as a place to live and gather.

sections.heading: From brewery to belonging
sections.body: A wayfinding-led identity honouring the brick-and-copper past.

cases.title: Maison Aurore
cases.intro: A fashion house identity poised between couture restraint and a dawn-lit optimism.

sections.heading: Light, made wearable
sections.body: Aurore means dawn. The system trades on soft gradients and generous space.

cases.title: Lumara Hotels
cases.intro: A luxury hospitality identity designed to feel quiet, warm, and unmistakably modern.

sections.heading: Understated luxury, end to end
sections.body: Every touchpoint was tuned for calm.

cases.title: Soluna Labs
cases.intro: A deep-tech startup brand engineered for trust — precise, technical, and quietly futuristic.

sections.heading: Make the complex feel inevitable
sections.body: A monochrome system with a single electric-green accent.

cases.title: Nexera Robotics
cases.intro: Brand identity for an industrial robotics company — engineered precision with a human touch.

sections.heading: Precision, made approachable
sections.body: A confident mark and a technical grid system for a robotics leader.

cases.title: Riixo
cases.intro: Recovery-wear product brand — performance energy balanced with clinical trust.

sections.heading: Built for the comeback
sections.body: Bold athletic identity for a compression-wear startup.

cases.title: ScoutX
cases.intro: Product platform UI for a talent-scouting app — data-dense, yet calm.

sections.heading: Signal over noise
sections.body: An interface that makes scouting data feel effortless.

cases.title: Gathr
cases.intro: Gifting brand campaign and packaging system — warmth you can unbox.

sections.heading: Engineered to delight
sections.body: A modular gifting system and launch content.

cases.title: Steggys
cases.intro: Playful F&B identity with a prehistoric wink — bold, friendly, instantly recognisable.

sections.heading: Big character, small package
sections.body: A mascot-led identity built for a fast-casual brand.

cases.title: Why most rebrands fail before the first sketch
cases.author: Hasaka
cases.excerpt: Strategy debt is the silent killer of brand projects. Here is how to spot it before you ever open the design tool.
cases.body: Most rebrands don't fail in execution — they fail in the gap between what a business says it is and what it actually does. When we skip the uncomfortable strategy conversations, we end up decorating a problem instead of solving it. This piece walks through the questions we ask before any visual work begins.

cases.title: How a visual language holds a brand together
cases.author: Studio Notes
cases.excerpt: A logo is a handshake; a system is the relationship. Notes on building identities that survive contact with the real world.
cases.body: Consistency isn't sameness. The strongest brand systems give teams a clear set of rules and the confidence to break them. We look at the components that make a visual language durable across 20+ markets.

cases.title: On craft, generative AI and the future of brand design
cases.author: Studio Notes
cases.excerpt: Where the tools end and taste begins — a conversation about staying useful in an AI-saturated industry.
cases.body: Generative tools collapse the cost of producing options. That makes judgement — knowing which option is right — the scarce, valuable skill. We talk through how the studio integrates AI without outsourcing its point of view.

cases.title: Notes on colour, type and the discipline of restraint
cases.author: Hasaka
cases.excerpt: The hardest part of a system is everything you choose to leave out.
cases.body: Restraint reads as confidence. A short field note on editing palettes and type ramps down to the essentials.

cases.title: Inside the Lumara Hotels identity system
cases.author: Hasaka
cases.excerpt: A behind-the-scenes look at building calm into every hospitality touchpoint.
cases.body: From wayfinding to the booking flow, we tuned every detail for quiet. A short case note on the decisions behind Lumara.

cases.title: Motion as a brand's second voice
cases.author: Studio Notes
cases.excerpt: Static identities are only half the story. How movement communicates personality.
cases.body: Timing, easing, and rhythm carry as much meaning as colour and type. An argument for treating motion as a core part of identity, not an afterthought.
